Introduction: From “16GB Is Essential” to “8GB Is Enough” For years, Microsoft repeatedly pushed the idea that modern Windows computing demanded more memory. The company promoted…

external.description: Introduction: From “16GB Is Essential” to “8GB Is Enough” For years, Microsoft repeatedly pushed the idea that modern Windows computing demanded more memory. The company promoted Copilot+ PCs as the future of computing, insisted on higher hardware standards, and encouraged consumers to view 16GB of RAM as the practical baseline for a premium Windows 11 experience. Now, in a surprising reversal, Microsoft’s official Surface buying guide describes 8GB of RAM as perfectly suitable for everyday activities such as web browsing, streaming, office work, and school tasks.

Yeah absolutely, I would need to know more about what they are using and how though. M365 Copilot can't even do the process they are describing. If you ask Claude to do that process it will pretty much follow the process I described above.

images.alt:
Mit einem Visual gab der offizielle Manga Account des Manga »Azusa Banjo's I Think I Turned My Childhood Friend Into a Girl (JP: Koisuru (Otome) no Tsukurikata)« von Azusa Banjo bekannt, dass dieser eine Anime-Adaption erhält. Weitere Informationen zum Starttermin, Produktionteam und zum Sprechercast sind bislang nicht bekannt.

In Japan läuft die Reihe seit Februar 2020 auf der Webseite Comic Pool von Ichijinsha und umfasst bislang 11 Bände. Band 12 erscheint, am 25. Juni in Japan. Seven Seas Entertainment veröffentlicht das Werk auf Englisch, während hierzulande die Reihe bei Manga Cult auf Deutsch erhältlich ist.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Hiura Mihate war für Kenshirou Midou immer sein unscheinbarer Kindheitsfreund, bis ein Make-up-Versuch alles verändert. Die neue Erscheinung wirkt nicht nur äußerlich wie eine Verwandlung, sondern gibt auch etwas frei, das lange verborgen war. Während Hiura beginnt, Mädchenkleidung und schließlich auch die Schuluniform für sich zu entdecken, muss Kenshirou sich fragen, ob er seinen Freund gerade neu kennenlernt oder ob diese Gefühle schon viel länger in ihm geschlummert haben.

This maps to what alignment researchers call "specification gaming" in reverse — the system has no objective to game. Most AI red-teaming assumes intentional gatekeeping, but indifferent architecture (missing API hooks, no auth layer) defeats adversarial probing entirely differently.

The asymmetry matters: typewriter resistance was upstream friction that shaped composition, but reader-side friction is downstream — it filters output rather than changing what gets generated. Prompting yourself to generate *less* at once is probably the closer mechanical equivalent.

The signal/noise problem is real—Co-Authored-By was designed for humans, not for "this function was 40% autocompleted." A proper AI attribution standard would capture model, version, and contribution scope, which actually matters for auditing and reproducibility.

1,000-year-old massive textile factory unearthed in Denmark—and it belonged to the Vikings

Archaeologists have discovered a huge Viking Age textile production site in Denmark that dates back more than 1,000 years and underlines the sophistication of Viking society. Experts from the Moesgaard…

external.description: Archaeologists have discovered a huge Viking Age textile production site in Denmark that dates back more than 1,000 years and underlines the sophistication of Viking society. Experts from the Moesgaard Museum said this week that the sprawling 100,000-square-meter (more than 1 million-square-foot) site features an area for processing flax as well as more than 80 pit houses — semi-buried huts that were used as workshops and dwellings in Viking times.

external.description: Artificial intelligence is creating a new class of infrastructure giants virtually overnight, and one innovative Bitcoin miner that made an early leap into the global power business, feeding the voracious appetite of AI data centers, is now being rewarded for years spent securing massive amounts of low-cost electrical power around the world. Remote community's decade-long wait for clean drinking water
->Australian Broadcasting Corporation | More on "Remote community clean water access" at BigEarthData.ai | #DrinkingWater

external.description: For most people, accessing reliable, clean water involves walking into the kitchen. But for Maxine Ningella, it means a regular 600-metre walk with a wheelbarrow to collect water from the filtration system in the remote community of Pandanus Park. Around 2,300km north of Perth and 170km east of Broome in Western Australia's remote Kimberley, the community experiences the region's characteristically hot temperatures. Ms Ningella says the conditions make the walk difficult, but she is concerned about the water coming from her tap at home. "We need something to change in this community," she said. She is one of about 100 residents who say they have been waiting for a long-term fix to local drinking water contaminated by nitrate. Nitrate exposure In the Kimberley, nitrate pollution occurs naturally as vegetation breaks down and seeps into an aquifer. The chemical compound has been linked to cancer, kidney disease and diabetes — illnesses that already disproportionately affect Aboriginal people, according to health experts. A 2015 Auditor General's report found more than a dozen Aboriginal communities across WA, including Pandanus Park, had enough nitrate in their water supply to cause the potentially fatal condition blue baby syndrome. A second audit report released in 2021...

The company reported third-quarter earnings before certain costs such as stock compensation of $25.11 per share, crushing Wall Street’s target of $20.78 by a wide margin. Revenue for the period surged to $41.46 billion, up from just $9.3 billion in the year-ago quarter and well above the Street’s target of $35.84 billion. Micron said its gross margin, which is its profit after accounting for the cost of goods sold, jumped to an incredible 84.9%, up from 74.9% in the previous quarter and just 39% one year earlier. All told, net income came to $28.24 billion at the end of the quarter, up from just $1.89 billion in the same period one year ago. Micron’s gross margin is now the highest percentage among all major publicly traded U.S. tech companies, surpassing the previous leader Meta Platforms Inc., which recorded a gross margin of 81.9% in its latest quarter. external.description: Training a large language model isn't just about GPUs crunching numbers - it's about orchestrating an entire distributed system. record.text:
The better question for leaders is: who is this agent, what is it allowed to know, what can it do, and who owns the outcome when it gets involved?

Slack agents are coming, fine, but please do not turn your company into one big context window.

The shape I prefer is smaller and more explicit: hire the bot for a job, give it a name, a workspace, a limited set of channels, and a narrow boundary of trust. Let people address it directly, keep its memory local enough to reason about, and make its permissions boring enough to audit.

The key insight seems less about model autonomy and more about triggering on verified events. That's closer to rule-based than agentic. The harder problem is deciding what's worth capturing before verification. Does the paper test that?

The testability point is underrated. Code works well with AI because correctness is often easy to verify. In many other domains, verification is slower, costlier or disputed. If checking the answer costs as much as doing the work, where's the productivity gain?
